focus: KQ1+KQ2: OpenCode agent/command resolution path and deep-loop YAML dispatch-vs-orchestrate boundary
noveltyJustification: First iteration; 4 of 5 findings fully new (agent-selection source, two-role contract, native-vs-CLI dispatch enforcement, conflation cues plus empirical leak), 1 partially new (agents-as-files mapping).

summary: opencode.json defines no agents/models/bindings; agent selection is a host/runtime decision
13 agents are file-defined under .opencode/agents; command markdown declares its own orchestrator as prose
Two-role contract: @general orchestrates markdown+YAML; fresh @deep-research leaf runs each iteration (agent_config leaf_only:true, model:opus)
step_dispatch_iteration is the handoff boundary; native=soft YAML prose dispatch, CLI=subprocess-enforced. Native is configured default.
Conflation cues: Phase-0 identity priming, prose-only native dispatch, Task-tool grant-not-mandate, prompt-pack leaf-instruction leak into orchestrator
AGENTS.md section 8 governs Task-tool delegation, not command-to-agent binding
Native dispatch directive is YAML prose with no runtime assertion that a fresh sub-agent context was spawned
Leaf-only invariants forbid the leaf from dispatching sub-agents; the trailing dispatch scaffolding in the leaf prompt violates role separation
This iteration-001 leaf dispatch literally ended with call-the-task-tool-with-subagent scaffolding meant for an orchestrator, empirical confirmation of the prompt-pack emission leak
Code graph was stale/error at session start, so structural queries were skipped in favor of direct Read of named asset paths

detail: agent-resolution logic could mean host runtime source code (not in repo); in-scope interpretation = contract surface consumed by runtime
resolution: used contract surface; host binary code flagged as out-of-repo follow-up
compensated with direct Read/Glob of named asset paths

reason: it is Task-tool delegation guidance, not command binding
